meta.note: Uruguay + Paraguay + Mexico sweep, published books. Gracias por el fuego already existed -> Uruguay ban-add. Held: El guardaespaldas (UY, a short story seized in a weekly, not a book), San Fernando (PY, primarily a performance/staging ban), La sombra del caudillo (MX, the documented ban was of the film). Mexico's modern entries are court-ordered modifications in moral-damage suits (restricted), not outright bans. Deduped against existing UY/PY/MX records.

entries.title: Gracias por el fuego
entries.description_ban: Benedetti's novel about a son's plot against a corrupt patriarch symbolising the Uruguayan ruling class; prohibited in Uruguay after the June 1973 civic-military coup. Adds a Uruguay ban to the existing record.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Adds a Uruguay ban; documented via Spanish Wikipedia and Planeta Uruguay.

entries.title: La agonía de los aché-guayakí
entries.description_ban: Anthropological denunciation of the genocide of the Aché people; its circulation was suppressed under Stroessner and co-author Bartomeu Meliá was expelled from Paraguay in 1976.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented by Religión Digital (medium confidence).

entries.title: Vivir como hermanos
entries.description_ban: Biblical reflections from the Christian Agrarian Leagues; seized by the Stroessner authorities as subversive, and the Jesuit author was kidnapped and exiled to Argentina in May 1972.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific seizure; documented by Última Hora.

entries.title: Historia de los pobres del Paraguay
entries.description_ban: History of Paraguay's poor labelled 'communist literature'; all copies were pulled from publishers and bookshops and the author exiled. She later recovered 345 confiscated copies via the Archivo del Terror.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific confiscation; documented by Última Hora.

entries.title: Los hijos de Sánchez
entries.description_ban: The Sociedad Mexicana de Geografía y Estadística filed a 1965 complaint calling the FCE edition 'obscene' and 'anti-Mexican'; the suit failed but the furore forced out the FCE director and the book was withdrawn from the state publisher.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific challenge/withdrawal; documented by Biblioteca de México and Letras Libres.

entries.title: Los días terrenales
entries.description_ban: After fierce attacks from the Mexican Communist Party and the left over its critique of Stalinism, Revueltas withdrew the novel from circulation — a title-specific withdrawal under ideological pressure.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific withdrawal; documented by the Enciclopedia de la Literatura en México and Milenio (medium confidence).
entries.source_name: Enciclopedia de la Literatura en México

entries.title: Historia general de las cosas de Nueva España
entries.description_ban: By a 1577 royal cédula, Felipe II ordered the confiscation of all of Sahagún's manuscripts (the Florentine Codex) and prohibited their circulation, fearing the detailed record of Mexica religion would sustain idolatry in colonial New Spain.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific colonial ban by royal decree; documented by the text of the Real cédula and Wikipedia.

entries.title: La casa blanca de Peña Nieto
entries.description_ban: MVS owner Joaquín Vargas sued over Carmen Aristegui's prologue; a Mexico City court (2016) ordered every edition to carry an excerpt of the sentence in the prologue — a court-ordered alteration of a specific title.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Court-ordered modification of a specific title; documented by El Informador and Aristegui Noticias.

entries.title: La jefa: vida pública y privada de Marta Sahagún de Fox
entries.description_ban: In 2005 a Mexican judge ordered Wornat to modify a chapter concerning illicit-enrichment accusations against Marta Sahagún's sons — a court-ordered partial restriction of a specific title.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Court-ordered modification of a specific title; documented by Diario Judicial and SinEmbargo (medium confidence).
